children.heading: National Strategy
children.label: a National Strategy
children.text: Not later than 9 months after , and based upon the previous and ongoing security assessments conducted by the Department and the Department of Transportation, the Secretary, consistent with and as required by section 114(t) of title 49, shall develop and implement the modal plan for public transportation, entitled the “National Strategy for Public Transportation Security”.August 3, 20071See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: minimize security threats to public transportation systems; and

children.label: B
children.text: maximize the abilities of public transportation systems to mitigate damage resulting from terrorist attack or other major incident.

children.label: A
children.text: use established and ongoing public transportation security assessments as the basis of the National Strategy for Public Transportation Security; and

children.label: B
children.text: consult with all relevant stakeholders, including public transportation agencies, nonprofit labor organizations representing public transportation employees, emergency responders, public safety officials, and other relevant parties.

children.heading: Contents
children.label: c Contents
children.text: In the National Strategy for Public Transportation Security, the Secretary shall describe prioritized goals, objectives, policies, actions, and schedules to improve the security of public transportation.
the identification of, and a plan to address, gaps and unnecessary overlaps in the roles, responsibilities, and authorities of Federal agencies; and

a process for coordinating existing or future security strategies and plans for public transportation, including the National Infrastructure Protection Plan required by Homeland Security Presidential Directive–7; Executive Order No. 13416: Strengthening Surface Transportation Security dated ; the Memorandum of Understanding between the Department and the Department of Transportation on Roles and Responsibilities dated ; and subsequent annexes and agreements.December 5, 2006September 28, 2004

children.heading: Adequacy of existing plans and strategies
children.label: e Adequacy of existing plans and strategies
children.text: In developing the National Strategy for Public Transportation Security, the Secretary shall use relevant existing risk assessments and strategies developed by the Department or other Federal agencies, including those developed or implemented pursuant to section 114(t) of title 49 or Homeland Security Presidential Directive–7.1

children.heading: Funding
children.label: f Funding
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $2,000,000 for fiscal year 2008.
